introSection: Tower of Terror II was a steel launched shuttle roller coaster located at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, Australia. It was the 5th fastest roller coaster in the world before its closure in 2019, being over taken by Red Force at Ferrari Land in 2017. Tower of Terror II was one of only two Reverse Freefall Coaster models designed by Intamin. Superman: Escape From Krypton at Six Flags Magic Mountain was the only remaining one left in operation until its closure in 2024.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Tower of Terror opened on 23 January 1997. In February, the top speed was verified as a record-breaking.

In August 2010, Dreamworld announced that it would be flipping the car around backwards and a tunnel would be added. The ride reopened only a few weeks later. The name changed from Tower of Terror to Tower of Terror II.

On 23 October 2019, it was announced that the ride had reached the end of its service life. The attraction would close permanently on 3 November 2019. Giant Drop, which uses the same structure as Tower of Terror II, continues to operate to this day.

The demolition of Tower of Terror II began in March 2020 with the removal of some track pieces. There is a remnant of the coaster, which is one of the pieces of track attached to the tower.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Tower of Terror II gave riders the feeling of weightlessness for 6.5 seconds.

fullText:
Tower of Terror II was a steel launched shuttle roller coaster located at Dreamworld in Coomera, Queensland, Australia. It was the 5th fastest roller coaster in the world before its closure in 2019, being over taken by Red Force at Ferrari Land in 2017. Tower of Terror II was one of only two Reverse Freefall Coaster models designed by Intamin. Superman: Escape From Krypton at Six Flags Magic Mountain was the only remaining one left in operation until its closure in 2024.

Tower of Terror opened on 23 January 1997. In February, the top speed was verified as a record-breaking.

In August 2010, Dreamworld announced that it would be flipping the car around backwards and a tunnel would be added. The ride reopened only a few weeks later. The name changed from Tower of Terror to Tower of Terror II.

On 23 October 2019, it was announced that the ride had reached the end of its service life. The attraction would close permanently on 3 November 2019. Giant Drop, which uses the same structure as Tower of Terror II, continues to operate to this day.

The demolition of Tower of Terror II began in March 2020 with the removal of some track pieces. There is a remnant of the coaster, which is one of the pieces of track attached to the tower.

Tower of Terror II gave riders the feeling of weightlessness for 6.5 seconds.

Grey track and supports.
